examples.label: Derive \(\sin3\theta\)EXAMPLE 35
examples.question_text: Express \(\sin3\theta\) in terms of powers of \(\sin\theta\).Reveal solutionLet \(c=\cos\theta\), \(s=\sin\theta\). By De Moivre, \(\cos3\theta+i\sin3\theta=(c+is)^3\).Expand: \((c+is)^3=c^3+3c^2is+3ci^2s^2+i^3s^3\).\(=c^3-3cs^2+i(3c^2s-s^3)\).Imaginary part: \(\sin3\theta=3c^2s-s^3\).Use \(c^2=1-s^2\): \(\sin3\theta=3(1-s^2)s-s^3\)..\(\sin3\theta=3\sin\theta-4\sin^3\theta\)

examples.label: Express \(\cos^3\theta\)EXAMPLE 36

examples.label: Inverse by adjointEXAMPLE 8
examples.question_text: For \(A=\begin{bmatrix}1&5&0\\-3&2&1\\1&2&1\end{bmatrix}\), use \[ \operatorname{adj}(A)=\begin{bmatrix}0&-5&5\\4&1&-1\\-8&3&17\end{bmatrix},\quad \det(A)=20. \] Find \(A^{-1}\).Reveal solutionUse \(A^{-1}=\frac1{\det(A)}\operatorname{adj}(A)\)..\(A^{-1}=\frac1{20}\begin{bmatrix}0&-5&5\\4&1&-1\\-8&3&17\end{bmatrix}\)
examples.solution_text: Use \(A^{-1}=\frac1{\det(A)}\operatorname{adj}(A)\)..\(A^{-1}=\frac1{20}\begin{bmatrix}0&-5&5\\4&1&-1\\-8&3&17\end{bmatrix}\)

examples.label: Determinant of inverseTRY
examples.question_text: If \(\det(A)=5\), what is \(\det(A^{-1})\)?Reveal solutionSince \(AA^{-1}=I\), \(\det(A)\det(A^{-1})=\det(I)=1\)..\(\det(A^{-1})=\frac15\)

examples.label: Cramer's ruleEXAMPLE 9
examples.question_text: Solve \[ 4x+5y=2,\quad 11x+y+2z=3,\quad x+5y+2z=1 \] using Cramer's rule.Reveal solutionThe coefficient matrix is \(A=\begin{bmatrix}4&5&0\\11&1&2\\1&5&2\end{bmatrix}\), and \(\det(A)=-132\ne0\).\(\det(A_x)=-36\), \(\det(A_y)=-24\), \(\det(A_z)=12\).Divide each by \(\det(A)\)..\(x=\frac3{11},\ y=\frac2{11},\ z=-\frac1{11}\)

examples.label: Inverse with parameterPRACTICE 2
examples.question_text: For \(A=\begin{bmatrix}2&k-2;\\3&k;\end{bmatrix}\), state when \(A\) is invertible.Reveal solutionCompute \(ad-bc=2k-3(k-2)=6-k\).The matrix is invertible when this is not zero..\(A\) is invertible when \(k\ne6\)

examples.label: Cofactor expansionPRACTICE 3
examples.question_text: For \[ A=\begin{bmatrix}2&1&3&3\\1&0&1&1\\0&2&2&0\\0&1&2&3\end{bmatrix}, \] evaluate \(\det(A)\).Reveal solutionExpanding carefully, the determinant is \(-2\).A good route is to expand along the first column, since it has two zeros..\(\det(A)=-2\)

examples.label: Reciprocal substitutionPRACTICE 4
examples.question_text: Solve \[ \frac1x+\frac2y+\frac1z=1,\quad \frac3x+\frac4y+\frac1z=5,\quad \frac8x+\frac6y+\frac7z=0. \] Reveal solutionLet \(X=\frac1x\), \(Y=\frac1y\), and \(Z=\frac1z\).Solve \(\begin{bmatrix}1&2&1\\3&4&1\\8&6&7\end{bmatrix}\begin{bmatrix}X\\Y\\Z\end{bmatrix}=\begin{bmatrix}1\\5\\0\end{bmatrix}\).This gives \(X=1,\ Y=1,\ Z=-2\)..\(x=1,\ y=1,\ z=-\frac12\)
